text:
“We never asked for a cease-fire, and we have never asked even for negotiation,” he said. “We are ready to defend ourselves as long as it takes.”
Mr. Trump said in a phone interview with NBC News on Saturday that “Iran wants to make a deal, and I don’t want to make it because the terms aren’t good enough yet.”
The International Energy Agency shared more details on Sunday morning about government efforts to release 400 million barrels of oil into the market from reserves around the world.
Member countries in Asia, a region that is especially reliant on oil from the Persian Gulf, plan to start putting oil into the market “immediately,” while those in the Americas and Europe plan to follow at the end of March, the agency said. In all, the I.E.A. said it had received detailed release plans for about two-thirds of the 400 million barrels.
Anushka Patil and Rebecca F. Elliott contributed reporting.
title: Iran War Live Updates: ‘No Guarantees’ Oil Prices Drop Soon, U.S. Official Says
